topic: Areas of Inquiry: Music for Dramatic Impact, Movement and Entertainment
subject: music

items.q: Which chords form a plagal cadence?
items.why: Students confuse plagal (IV-I) with perfect (V-I) because both end on I. The 'amen' at the end of hymns is the classic plagal cadence - subdominant to tonic, with a softer ecclesiastical feel.

items.q: Which orchestral family does the saxophone belong to?
items.misconception: Brass (because it is made of brass).
items.why: Students classify by material. Instrument families are defined by SOUND PRODUCTION: woodwinds use a reed or air across an edge. The sax uses a reed, so despite the brass body, it's woodwind.

items.q: What is a balanced antecedent-consequent phrase?
items.correct: Two phrases of equal length where the first sounds open (question) and the second resolves (answer).
items.misconception: Any two phrases of similar style.
items.random_wrong: A long melody with no breaks.
items.why: Students miss the FUNCTIONAL relationship. The antecedent typically ends on an imperfect cadence (open); the consequent answers with a perfect cadence (closed). Length matches; cadences contrast.

items.q: In what year was the world music label adopted by indie labels?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1987. “1972” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many areas of inquiry frame IBDP Music since 2020?
items.why: Exact answer: 4 areas. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
